title: Span Creation and Lifecycle
interaction_flow: DDTrace → SpanStack: create new root SpanStack, switch active stack
DDTrace → RootSpanData: instantiate with id, parent=null, stack ref
DDTrace → SpanStack: push new SpanData onto active stack (inherits service/env/version from parent)
DDTrace → SpanData: instantiate with id, parent=active_span, stack ref
NOTE: if no active span exists, start_span behaves like start_trace_span (creates new root stack)
Caller → SpanData: $span->links[] = SpanLink (for distributed trace links)
Caller → Span: setTag(key, value) — routes special tags (ERROR, SERVICE_NAME, RESOURCE_NAME, SPAN_TYPE, HTTP_URL, HTTP_STATUS_CODE) to internalSpan fields
Caller → Span: setError(Throwable|bool|null) — sets meta[error.msg/type/stack] and hasError flag
Caller → Span: setMetric(key, float) — writes to internalSpan->metrics[]
DDTrace → SpanStack: pop top user-span, record finish time, compute duration
DDTrace → SpanData: invoke onClose[] handlers in reverse order
Span → DDTrace: active_span() — check if this span is on top
alt span NOT on top (out-of-order finish)
DDTrace → SpanStack: close all spans above target span (returns count closed)
NOTE: only effective if span not yet flushed; may not work with auto_flush_enabled on root spans
Caller → DDTrace: switch_stack(SpanData|SpanStack|null): null|false|SpanStack — switch active span context
Caller → DDTrace: root_span(): RootSpanData|null — get root span of active stack
key_relationships: RootSpanData extends SpanData (adds: origin, samplingPriority, propagatedTags, parentId, gitMetadata, inferredSpan)
SpanData has onClose: Closure[] — invoked on close, not on drop
DDTrace ext functions operate on thread-local SpanStack
close_spans_until_semantics: Closes all spans on the active stack above (and not including) the given parent span. Returns false if span is not found, or the count of spans closed. Used for out-of-order finishing.
important_notes: Times are in seconds (float) for API input, stored/returned in nanoseconds internally
start_span with no active span === start_trace_span (distributed tracing headers applied)
Span::finish() logs a warning if close_spans_until closes multiple spans at once
update_span_duration is unreliable on root spans with auto_flush_enabled
onClose handlers called in reverse order; cleared on close or drop
try_drop_span(SpanData) drops span without metrics, fails if has non-dropped children or distributed links
